pages.raw_text:
The Washington Equal Suffrage
association. of which Mrs. Emma
Smith Devoe is president, has pro-
crossed beyond the stage of type
written publicity and is now issu-
Ing a neat printed bulletin from the
Seattle headquarters. Among other
matters of interest to the COM
Nunity is the following:


When the legislature convenes in
Olympia in January quiet delega-
tion of women will be on hand to
keep a watchful eye on the inter-
ests affecting our bill. 1t will be
q very quiet, unobstrusive delega-
tion, with no band music or BAN
ners, no street parades and no lob
bying. Our representatives will
simply be there to see that prom
Uses made us are not forgotten in
the stress of other business, and
that our bill IS fairly and sqarely
presented 1t is not certain that
our presence there will prevent de-
feat, but the absent are always
more easily forgotten than the
present; besides, the argument so
dear to the heart of the politicians
who are opposed to granting
women the ballot, namely, women
do not want to VOTE" can not hon.
estly be offered when there is a
quiet but determined group OF
women In the city representing a
large body Of women who have Pc
titioned the legislature for this
amendment.


Mrs. May Arkwright Hutton of
Spokane, whose intimate knowi-
edge Of politics and statecraft, com.
bined with her splendid executive
ability, make her an invaluable ally
where legislative measures are con.
cerned. has engaged rooms at the
Mitchell. from whence she and Mrs.
N. LaReine Baker will do turns at
\ night and day vigil over the
hopeful infant which we will pre-.
sent to the body of lawmakers,


At the Horn residence a suite OF
fooms has been engaged, where
Etate President Mrs. DeVoe and a
corps Of workers will keep before
The minds OF the legislators the
Fact that the women OF Washington
IRE thoroughly in earnest In theft
Remand for the ballot. We have
received many pledges of support
Tor our maasure-sometiin more
than mere passive support.
